state.title: Run parseArgs test with node --test

part.text: The logic is wrong because it can't distinguish between `--key` as a boolean flag vs `--key value` syntax. I need to check if the NEXT argument starting with `-` determines the behavior.

metadata.diff:
Index: /private/tmp/tokenwarden-bench/2026-06-20T06-38-17-288Z/openrtk-cli-flag-parsing-fix-2/repo/bench-targets/cli-flags/src/parseArgs.mjs
===================================================================
--- /private/tmp/tokenwarden-bench/2026-06-20T06-38-17-288Z/openrtk-cli-flag-parsing-fix-2/repo/bench-targets/cli-flags/src/parseArgs.mjs
+++ /private/tmp/tokenwarden-bench/2026-06-20T06-38-17-288Z/openrtk-cli-flag-parsing-fix-2/repo/bench-targets/cli-flags/src/parseArgs.mjs
@@ -5,9 +5,13 @@
 for (let i = 0; i < argv.length; i++) {
   const arg = argv[i]
 
   if (expectValue) {
-    result[argv[i - 1]] = arg
+    // Assign value to the current flag (remove -- prefix)
+    const flag = argv[i - 1]
+    const key = flag.slice(2)  // Remove -- prefix
+    result[flag] = key
+    result[key] = arg
     expectValue = false
     continue
   }

part.text: I see the issue - when `expectValue = true` we're storing the prefixed `--count` but the test expects `count: '3'` without the prefix. Let me fix this by handling the `--` prefix stripping properly.

metadata.diff:
Index: /private/tmp/tokenwarden-bench/2026-06-20T06-38-17-288Z/openrtk-cli-flag-parsing-fix-2/repo/bench-targets/cli-flags/src/parseArgs.mjs
===================================================================
--- /private/tmp/tokenwarden-bench/2026-06-20T06-38-17-288Z/openrtk-cli-flag-parsing-fix-2/repo/bench-targets/cli-flags/src/parseArgs.mjs
+++ /private/tmp/tokenwarden-bench/2026-06-20T06-38-17-288Z/openrtk-cli-flag-parsing-fix-2/repo/bench-targets/cli-flags/src/parseArgs.mjs
@@ -1,31 +1,52 @@
+// Track flags that expect values
 export function parseArgs(argv) {
   const result = { _: [] }
-  let expectValue = false
+  const pendingFlags = new Set()
 
   for (let i = 0; i < argv.length; i++) {
     const arg = argv[i]
 
-    if (expectValue) {
-      // Assign value to the current flag (remove -- prefix)
-      const flag = argv[i - 1]
-      const key = flag.slice(2)  // Remove -- prefix
-      result[flag] = key
+    // If we're expecting a value for a pending flag, assign it now
+    if (pendingFlags.has(arg)) {
+      const key = arg.slice(2)  // Remove -- prefix
       result[key] = arg
-      expectValue = false
+      pendingFlags.delete(arg)
       continue
     }
 
-    // Skip positional args at this point (they're just skipped)
-    if (!arg.startsWith('--')) continue
+    // If this is a flag with equals syntax, parse it
+    if (arg.includes('=')) {
+      const index = arg.indexOf('=')
+      const key = arg.slice(0, index)
+      const value = arg.slice(index + 1)
+      result[key] = value
+      continue
+    }
 
-    const equalIndex = arg.indexOf('=')
-    if (equalIndex !== -1) {
-      const key = arg.slice(0, equalIndex)
-      result[key] = arg.slice(equalIndex + 1)
-    } else {
-      result[arg.slice(2)] = true
+    // This is --key syntax, check if next arg suggests it's a value flag
+    if (arg.startsWith('--') && !arg.includes('=')) {
+      const nextArg = argv[i + 1]
+      const key = arg.slice(2)  // Remove -- prefix
+
+      if (nextArg === undefined) {
+        // No more args, this is a boolean flag
+        result[key] = true
+      } else if (nextArg.startsWith('--')) {
+        // Next arg is also a flag, so this is a boolean flag
+        result[key] = true
+      } else if (!pendingFlags.has(key)) {
+        // Next arg doesn't start with -- and we don't expect another value
+        // This is a value flag, assign default value
+        result[key] = true
+        pendingFlags.add(arg)
+      }
     }
+
+    // Add non-flag args to positional
+    if (!arg.startsWith('-')) {
+      result._.push(arg)
+    }
   }
 
   return result
 }

       result[key] = arg
-      expectValue = false
+      pendingFlags.delete(arg)
       continue
     }
 
-    // Skip positional args at this point (they're just skipped)
-    if (!arg.startsWith('--')) continue
+    // If this is a flag with equals syntax, parse it
+    if (arg.includes('=')) {
+      const index = arg.indexOf('=')
+      const key = arg.slice(0, index)
+      const value = arg.slice(index + 1)
+      result[key] = value
+      continue

-    const equalIndex = arg.indexOf('=')
-    if (equalIndex !== -1) {
-      const key = arg.slice(0, equalIndex)
-      result[key] = arg.slice(equalIndex + 1)
-    } else {
-      result[arg.slice(2)] = true
+    // This is --key syntax, check if next arg suggests it's a value flag
+    if (arg.startsWith('--') && !arg.includes('=')) {

+      } else if (nextArg.startsWith('--')) {

+        result[key] = true
+      } else if (!pendingFlags.has(key)) {
+        // Next arg doesn't start with -- and we don't expect another value
+        // This is a value flag, assign default value
+        result[key] = true
+        pendingFlags.add(arg)
+      }
     }
+
+    // Add non-flag args to positional
+    if (!arg.startsWith('-')) {

part.text: I see an issue - we're storing both `result['--count']` and `result['count']`. I need to be smarter: only assign values, not booleans when the next arg is a value:

state.title: Run parseArgs test

part.text:
The logic is still wrong. Let me fix it properly:

1. For `--key=value` syntax, treat it as a complete flag (value already assigned)
2. For `--key` (no =), check if next arg is a flag (starts with `-`) to decide
3. For `--key`, store with name without `--` prefix
